The antibacterial performance of PLGA-OH-CATH30 MS was further evaluated us-
ing the  colony counting method. As shown in Figure 5B, it  was observed that with the
incubation time of MS and bacteria, more and more bacteria were killed, and the antibac-
terial efficiency gradually improved. The antibacterial efficiency of MS against Escherichia
coli had already reached over 85% after incubation for 4  h, while that against Staphylo-
coccus aureus was around 15%. However, after 24 h of incubation, the antibacterial effi-
ciency  against  all  three  types  of  bacteria  had  reached  over  90%.  This  may  be  because
Staphylococcus aureus can form a protective layer called a ‘biofilm’, which makes it more
difficult to be attacked by drugs and the immune system [34]. In line with previous studies
